name: lightning-python

metadata.name: lightning-python
metadata.summary: A Python client library for the Lightning data visualization server

documentation:
[![Build Status](https://travis-ci.org/lightning-viz/lightning-python.svg?branch=master)](https://travis-ci.org/lightning-viz/lightning-python)
[![Gitter](https://badges.gitter.im/Join%20Chat.svg)](https://gitter.im/lightning-viz/lightning?utm_source=badge&utm_medium=badge&utm_campaign=pr-badge&utm_content=badge)


Lightning python client
================

Python client for the [lightning](https://github.com/mathisonian/lightning) API

## installation
Install using:

```
pip install lightning-python
```
Compatible with Python 2.7 and 3.4. 

## usage

### creating a session

```python
from lightning import Lightning

lgn = Lightning(host="http://my-lightning-instance.herokuapp.com")

lgn.create_session()
lgn.create_session("provide an optional session name")
```

### creating a visualization
Methods are available for the default visualization types included with Lightning
```python
lgn.line([1,2,3,4,5,6,7,8,0,-2,2])
lgn.scatter([1,2,3],[2,9,4])
```

### setting options
Visualizations can be customized through optional parameters
```python
lgn.scatter([1,2,3],[2,9,4], label=[1,2,3], size=[5,10,20])
```
### using custom plots
For custom plots not included with the default set, specify by name and provide data as a dictionary
```python
lgn.plot(data={"series": [1,2,3]}, type='line')
```

## examples

See a collection of [IPython notebooks](http://nbviewer.ipython.org/github/lightning-viz/lightning-example-notebooks/tree/master/).

## complete documentation

Available [here](http://lightning-viz.github.io/lightning-python/).

## running tests

Requires [pytest](http://pytest.org/latest/)

Clone the repo and install the library locally:

```sh
$ pip install -e .
``` 

The tests need to be run against a lightning server. By default they expect
this to be found at `http://localhost:3000`.

To run the tests:

```
$ py.test
```

or with against a custom host url


```
$ py.test --host=http://mylightninghost.herokuapp.com
```

## help

We maintain a [chatroom](https://gitter.im/lightning-viz/lightning) on gitter. If there's no response there: file an issue or reach out on twitter ([@mathisonian](http://twitter.com/matisonian), [@thefreemanlab](http://twitter.com/thefreemanlab))

methods.name: lightning.visualization.VisualizationLocal.get_html
documentation:
Return html for this local visualization.

Assumes that Javascript has already been embedded,
to be used for rendering in notebooks.

methods.name: lightning.visualization.VisualizationLocal.save_html
documentation:
Save self-contained html to a file.

Parameters
----------
filename : str
    The filename to save to

functions.name: lightning.types.utils.check_property
documentation:
Check and parse a property with either a specific checking function
or a generic parser

functions.name: lightning.types.utils.check_coordinates
documentation:
Check and parse coordinates as either a single coordinate list [[r,c],[r,c]] or a
list of coordinates for multiple regions [[[r0,c0],[r0,c0]], [[r1,c1],[r1,c1]]]

functions.name: lightning.types.utils.check_color
documentation:
Check and parse color specs as either a single [r,g,b] or a list of
[[r,g,b],[r,g,b]...]

functions.name: lightning.types.utils.check_colormap
documentation: Check if cmap is one of the colorbrewer maps

functions.name: lightning.types.utils.check_size
documentation: Check and parse size specs as either a single [s] or a list of [s,s,s,...]

functions.name: lightning.types.utils.check_thickness
documentation: Check and parse thickness specs as either a single [s] or a list of [s,s,s,...]

functions.name: lightning.types.utils.check_index
documentation: Checks and parses an index spec, must be a one-dimensional array [i0, i1, ...]

functions.name: lightning.types.utils.check_alpha
documentation: Check and parse alpha specs as either a single [a] or a list of [a,a,a,...]

functions.name: lightning.types.utils.check_1d
documentation: Check and parse a one-dimensional spec as either a single [x] or a list of [x,x,x...]

functions.name: lightning.types.utils.polygon_to_mask
documentation:
Given a list of pairs of points which define a polygon, return a binary
mask covering the interior of the polygon with dimensions dim

functions.name: lightning.types.utils.polygon_to_points
documentation:
Given a list of pairs of points which define a polygon,
return a list of points interior to the polygon

methods.name: lightning.types.streaming.LineStreaming.clean
documentation:
Plot streaming one-dimensional series data as updating lines.

Plotting once returns a visualization on which 'append' can be called
to add new data in a streaming fashion. New lines will appear on the right.

```{image} line-streaming.png
```

# Parameters

series

: Input data for line plot, typically n series each of length m.
  Can also pass a list where each individual series is of a different length.

index

: Specify index for the x-axis of the line plot.

color

: Single rgb value or array to set line colors

group

: Single integer or array to set line colors via group assignment

size

: Single size or array to set line thickness

xaxis

: Label for x-axis

yaxis

: Label for y-axis

max_width

: The maximum number of time points to show before plot shifts.

methods.name: lightning.types.streaming.ScatterStreaming.clean
documentation:
Create a streaming scatter plot of x and y.

Plotting once returns a visualization on which 'append' can be called to add new data
in a streaming fashion. The opacity of old and new data is automatically set
to highlight the most recent data and fade old data away.

```{image} scatter-streaming.png
```

# Parameters

x, y

: Input data

values

: Values to set node colors via a linear scale

labels

: Array of text labels to set tooltips

color

: Single rgb value or array to set colors

group

: Single integer or array to set colors via groups

colormap

: Specification of color map, only colorbrewer types supported

size

: Single size or array to set point sizes

xaxis

: Label for x-axis

yaxis

: Label for y-axis

methods.name: lightning.types.three.Scatter3.clean
documentation:
Plot three-dimensional data as points.

```{image} scatter3.png
```

# Parameters

x, y, z

: Input data

color

: Single rgb value or array to set colors

group

: Single integer or array to set colors via groups

size

: Single size or array to set point sizes

alpha

: Single alpha value or array to set fill and stroke opacity

methods.name: lightning.types.three.Volume.clean
documentation:
Display a collection of images as a three-dimensional volume.

```{image} volume.png
```

# Parameters

imagedata

: Image or list of images as two dimensional (grayscale) or three dimensional (RGB) arrays.

methods.name: lightning.types.base.Base._clean_data
documentation:
Convert raw data into a dictionary with plot-type specific methods.

The result of the cleaning operation should be a dictionary.
If the dictionary contains a 'data' field it will be passed directly
(ensuring appropriate formatting). Otherwise, it should be a
dictionary of data-type specific array data (e.g. 'points', 
'timeseries'), which will be labeled appropriately
(see _check_unkeyed_arrays).

methods.name: lightning.types.base.Base._baseplot
documentation:
Base method for plotting data and images.

Applies a plot-type specific cleaning operation to generate
a dictionary with the data, then creates a visualization with the data. 
Expects a session and a type, followed by all plot-type specific
positional and keyword arguments, which will be handled by the clean
method of the given plot type.

If the dictionary contains only images, or only non-image data, 
they will be passed on their own. If the dictionary contains
both images and non-image data, the images will be appended
to the visualization.

methods.name: lightning.types.base.Base.update
documentation:
Base method for updating data.

Applies a plot-type specific cleaning operation, then
updates the data in the visualization.

methods.name: lightning.types.base.Base.append
documentation:
Base method for appending data.

Applies a plot-type specific cleaning operation, then
appends data to the visualization.

methods.name: lightning.types.base.Base._get_user_data
documentation: Base method for retrieving user data from a viz.

methods.name: lightning.types.images.Image.clean
documentation:
Display an array as an image.

```{image} image.png
```

# Parameters

imagedata

: Image as a two dimensional (grayscale) or three dimensional (RGB) array.

methods.name: lightning.types.images.ImagePoly.clean
documentation:
Display an array as an image with polygonal regions and region drawing.

```{image} image-poly.png
```

# Parameters

imagedata

: Image as a two dimensional (grayscale) or three dimensional (RGB) array.

polygons

: List of coordinates or list of list of coordinates of polygonal regions.
  Assumes array indexing (i.e. row/column), in the form \[[r,c],[r,c]\]
  for one polygon or \[\[[r0,c0],[r0,c0]\], \[[r1,c1],[r1,c1]\]\] for multiple regions.

xy

: Only if True treat coordinates as x/y positions instead of row/column indices

methods.name: lightning.types.images.ImagePoly._coords
documentation: Raw coordinates of regions retrieved from visualization user data.

methods.name: lightning.types.images.ImagePoly.polygons
documentation:
Coordinates of polygons as drawn on an image.

These coordinates can be drawn directly to an image using lighting.imagepoly
and should be in the exact same locations as they were drawn.

methods.name: lightning.types.images.ImagePoly.points
documentation:
Points contained in regions drawn on an image

Parameters
----------
z : int, optional, default=None
    Append a z-index to coordinates (yielding three dimensional coordinates)

methods.name: lightning.types.images.ImagePoly.masks
documentation:
Binary masks with regions filled in as 1s or 0s.

Parameters
----------
dims : array-like
    Specify the dimensions of the image containing the polygons

z : int, optional, default=None
    Use a z-index to insert regions into the appropriate slice if using
    three-dimensional volumes.

methods.name: lightning.types.images.Gallery.clean
documentation:
Display a collection of arrays as browsable images with thumbnails.

```{image} gallery.png
```

# Parameters

imagedata

: Image or list of images as two dimensional (grayscale) or three dimensional (RGB) arrays.

methods.name: lightning.main.Lightning.enable_ipython
documentation:
Enable plotting in the iPython notebook.

Once enabled, all lightning plots will be automatically produced
within the iPython notebook. They will also be available on
your lightning server within the current session.

methods.name: lightning.main.Lightning.disable_ipython
documentation:
Disable plotting in the iPython notebook.

After disabling, lightning plots will be produced in your lightning server,
but will not appear in the notebook.

methods.name: lightning.main.Lightning.create_session
documentation:
Create a lightning session.

Can create a session with the provided name, otherwise session name
will be "Session No." with the number automatically generated.

methods.name: lightning.main.Lightning.use_session
documentation:
Use the specified lightning session.

Specify a lightning session by id number. Check the number of an existing
session in the attribute lightning.session.id.

methods.name: lightning.main.Lightning.enable_local
documentation:
Enable a local mode.

Data is handled locally and embedded via templates.
Does not require a running Lightning server.
Useful for notebooks, and can be used offline.

methods.name: lightning.main.Lightning.disable_local
documentation: Disable local mode.

methods.name: lightning.main.Lightning.set_basic_auth
documentation: Set authenatication.

methods.name: lightning.main.Lightning.set_host
documentation:
Set the host for a lightning server.

Host can be local (e.g. http://localhost:3000), a heroku
instance (e.g. http://lightning-test.herokuapp.com), or
a independently hosted lightning server.

methods.name: lightning.main.Lightning.set_size
documentation:
Set a figure size using one of four options.

Convention is 'small': 400px, 'medium': 600px, 'large': 800px,
and 'full' will use the entire width

methods.name: lightning.main.Lightning.check_status
documentation: Check the server for status

methods.name: lightning.types.plots.Plot.clean
documentation:
Generic plotting function.

Provide arbitrary data and options objects as dictionaries,
and a plot type as a string. The data and options dictionary will be passed
directly to the plot, without any parsing or formatting,
so make sure it is of the appropriate for your visualization
(e.g. {"series": [1,2,3]} for a "line" visualization).

Most useful when providing data to custom visualizations, as opposed
to the included plot types (e.g. lightning.scatter, lightning.line, etc.)
which do automatic parsing and formatting.

Parameters
----------
data : dict
    Dictionary with data to plot

type : str
    Name of plot (e.g. 'line' or 'scatter')

methods.name: lightning.types.plots.Scatter.clean
documentation:
Plot two-dimensional data as points.

```{image} scatter.png
```

# Parameters

x, y

: Input data

values

: Values to set node colors via a linear scale

labels

: Array of text labels to set tooltips

color

: Single rgb value or array to set colors

group

: Single integer or array to set colors via groups

colormap

: Specification of color map, only colorbrewer types supported

size

: Single size or array to set point sizes

alpha

: Single alpha value or array to set fill and stroke opacity

xaxis

: Label for x-axis

yaxis

: Label for y-axis

tooltips

: Whether to show tooltips

zoom

: Whether to allow zooming

brush

: Whether to support brushing

methods.name: lightning.types.plots.Scatter.selected
documentation: Selected points from scatter plot as indices

methods.name: lightning.types.plots.Scatter.points
documentation: Selected points from scatter plot as x,y coordinates

methods.name: lightning.types.plots.Matrix.clean
documentation:
Visualize a dense matrix or table as a heat map.

```{image} matrix.png
```

# Parameters

matrix

: Two-dimensional array of matrix data

row_labels

: Array of rows to label columns

column_labels

: Array of strings to label columns

colormap

: Specification of color map, only colorbrewer types supported

numbers

: Whether to show numbers on cells

methods.name: lightning.types.plots.Adjacency.clean
documentation:
Visualize a sparse adjacency matrix.

```{image} adjacency.png
```

# Parameters

conn

: Input connectivity data as either a matrix or a list of links.
  Matrix can be binary or continuous valued. Links should contain
  either 2 elements per link (source, target),
  or 3 elements (source, target, value).

labels

: Text labels for each item (will label rows and columns)

group

: Single integer or array to set colors via groups

sort

: What to sort by, options are 'group' | 'degree'

numbers

: Whether to show numbers on cells

symmetric

: Whether to make links symmetrical

methods.name: lightning.types.plots.Line.clean
documentation:
Plot one-dimensional series data as lines.

```{image} line.png
```

# Parameters

series

: Input data for line plot, typically n series each of length m.
  Can also pass a list where each individual series is of a different length.

index

: Specify index for the x-axis of the line plot.

color

group

: Single integer or array to set line colors via group assignment

thickness

xaxis

yaxis

: Label for y-axis

zoom

: Whether to allow zooming

Create a force-directed network from connectivity.

```{image} force.png
```

# Parameters

conn

: Input connectivity data as either a matrix or a list of links.

: Whether to show tooltips

brush

methods.name: lightning.types.plots.Force.selected
documentation: Selected points from force plot

methods.name: lightning.types.plots.Circle.clean
documentation:
Create a circular graph from connectivity data.

```{image} circle.png
```

# Parameters

conn

: Input connectivity data as either a matrix or a list of links.
  Matrix can be binary or continuous valued. Links should contain
  either 2 elements per link (source, target),
  or 3 elements (source, target, value).

group

: Hierarchical group assignments, where m is
  the number of groups

color

: Single rgb value or array to set colors of top-level group,
  where k is the number of unique elements in the top-level group

labels

: Array of text labels to label nodes

values

labels

color

: Single rgb value or array to set node colors

group

: Single integer or array to set node colors via group assignment

colormap

: Specification of color map, only colorbrewer types supported

size

: Single size or array to set node sizes

tooltips

: Whether to show tooltips

zoom

: Whether to allow zooming

brush

: Whether to support brushing

Create a bundled node-link graph from spatial points and their connectivity.


# Parameters

x,y

: Input data for nodes (x,y coordinates)

conn

: Input connectivity data as either a matrix or a list of links.
  Matrix can be binary or continuous valued. Links should contain
  either 2 elements per link (source, target),
  or 3 elements (source, target, value).

values

: Values to set node colors via a linear scale

labels

: Array of text labels to set tooltips

color

: Single rgb value or array to set node colors

group

: Single integer or array to set node colors via group assignment

colormap

: Specification of color map, only colorbrewer types supported

size

: Single size or array to set node sizes

tooltips

: Whether to show tooltips

zoom

: Whether to allow zooming

brush

: Whether to support brushing

methods.name: lightning.types.plots.Map.clean
documentation:
Create a chloropleth map of the world or united states.

```{image} map.png
```

# Parameters

regions

: String identifiers for map regions, either length two strings (for states
  in a US map) or length three strings (for countries in a world map)

weights

: Values to use to color each region

colormap

: Specification of color map, only colorbrewer types supported

methods.name: lightning.types.plots.Histogram.clean
documentation:
Create a histogram.

```{image} histogram.png
```

# Parameters

values

: Values to plot a histogram of

bins

: Number of bins to used in the histogram. If unspecified
  will default to sqrt(len(values))
